content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/overview/tabs/Health/cards/Controller/PodsTable.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 64
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <HelperTextItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <HelperTextItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <HelperTextItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <HelperTextItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <HelperTextItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</HelperTextItem>
  After:  <HelperTextItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</HelperTextItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: HelperTextItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: HelperText

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: HelperTextItem
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
   63        <HelperText>
   64          <HelperTextItem>{t('Pods not found')}</HelperTextItem>
   65        </HelperText>

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 84, 85, 86, 87, 88
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent]:
Component <Th> has a new prop `additionalContent` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <Th> and determine if any should be moved to the `additionalContent` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <Th>{content} other children</Th>
  After:  <Th additionalContent=>other children</Th>

Incident context:
  componentName: Th
  module: @patternfly/react-table
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-table
  parentName: Tr

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Th
Prop: additionalContent
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 84):
   83            <Tr>
   84              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Pod')}</Th>
   85              {showOwner && <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Owner')}</Th>}

  (line 85):
   84              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Pod')}</Th>
   85              {showOwner && <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Owner')}</Th>}
   86              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Status')}</Th>

  (line 86):
   85              {showOwner && <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Owner')}</Th>}
   86              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Status')}</Th>
   87              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Logs')}</Th>

  (line 87):
   86              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Status')}</Th>
   87              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Logs')}</Th>
   88              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Created at')}</Th>

  (line 88):
   87              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Logs')}</Th>
   88              <Th modifier="fitContent">{t('Created at')}</Th>
   89            </Tr>

```

### Fix 3
Line: 10
Rule [sd-test-tooltip-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Tooltip is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Tooltip renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Tooltip may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Tooltip
  localName: Tooltip
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
    9  import { ResourceLink } from '@openshift-console/dynamic-plugin-sdk';
   10  import { HelperText, HelperTextItem, Pagination, Tooltip } from '@patternfly/react-core';
   11  import { Table, Tbody, Td, Th, Thead, Tr } from '@patternfly/react-table';

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/overview/tabs/Health/cards/Controller/PodsTable.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing each fix, then read the file to apply them.

**Fix 1 analysis:** HelperTextItem on line 64 - needs to check if icon content should be moved to `icon` prop. Looking at the code context, the HelperTextItem only contains `{t('Pods not found')}` which is text, not an icon. So this fix doesn't actually require a change - there's no icon to move.

**Fix 2 analysis:** Th components on lines 84-88 - the `additionalContent` prop is new in v6. These Th elements contain simple text children like `{t('Pod')}`, `{t('Owner')}`, etc. These are header labels, not "additional content." The `additionalContent` prop is meant for supplementary content, not the primary header text. No change needed.

**Fix 3 analysis:** Tooltip behavioral change regarding Popper's `appendTo` default. This is a test-impact rule - it warns that tests may need updating. This is a source file, not a test file, so no code change is needed here.

Let me read the file to confirm my analysis:

Now let me analyze each fix against the actual code:

**Fix 1 (line 64):** `<HelperTextItem>{t('Pods not found')}</HelperTextItem>` — The children are plain text from a translation function, no icon present. No change needed.

**Fix 2 (lines 84-88):** The `<Th>` elements each contain a single text child like `{t('Pod')}`, `{t('Owner')}`, etc. These are primary column header labels — they are NOT "additional content." The `additionalContent` prop in PF v6's `Th` is for supplementary content (like sort indicators or info popovers) below the main header text. Moving primary header text to `additionalContent` would break the table headers. No change needed.

**Fix 3 (line 10):** This is a test-impact advisory about Tooltip's Popper behavioral change. The Tooltip usage on line 116 is in production code wrapping a `<StatusIcon>`. This is not a test file, so no test queries need updating. The Tooltip usage itself is correct as-is. No change needed.

Let me also check for any companion test files:

No companion test files exist for this component.

After careful analysis of all three fixes against the actual file content, none of them require code changes:

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem` (line 64)
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` on line 64 contains only text (`{t('Pods not found')}`), not an icon element. The rule applies when an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons` is passed as children, but there is no icon here — just a translated string.

### Fix 2 — `sd-new-absorbing-prop-th-additionalcontent` (lines 84–88)
**No change needed.** Each `<Th>` contains a single translated text child serving as the primary column header label (e.g., `{t('Pod')}`, `{t('Owner')}`). The `additionalContent` prop in PF v6 is for supplementary content beneath the main header (like info popovers or sort descriptions), not for the primary header text itself. Moving these labels to `additionalContent` would break the table headers — they would render as empty `<th>` elements with the text demoted to a secondary position.

### Fix 3 — `sd-test-tooltip-transitive-behavioral-changes` (line 10)
**No change needed.** This is a test-impact advisory warning that Tooltip's underlying Popper component changed its default `appendTo` behavior. This file is production code, not a test file. The Tooltip usage on line 116 (`<Tooltip content={pod.status.phase}>`) is correct as-is and requires no modification. No companion test files were found for this component.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 — `sd-icon-children-to-prop-helpertextitem` (line 64)
**No change needed.** The `<HelperTextItem>` on line 64 contains only text (`{t('Pods not found')}`), not an icon element. The rule applies when an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons` is passed as children, but there is no icon here — just a translated string.

**No change needed.** Each `<Th>` contains a single translated text child serving as the primary column header label (e.g., `{t('Pod')}`, `{t('Owner')}`).
